name: Neighborhood Housing Partnership Of Greater Springfield, Inc.
description_short: Non-profit HUD-approved housing counselor providing free homebuyer education, financial coaching, and foreclosure intervention across Ohio.
description_long:
Neighborhood Housing Partnership of Greater Springfield (NHP) is a non-profit organization established to increase homeownership, promote housing rehabilitation, and support neighborhood revitalization across Clark, Champaign, and contiguous Ohio counties. As a chartered member of NeighborWorks America with an exemplary network rating, NHP operates as a HUD-approved housing counseling agency with all three counselors holding national HUD certifications.

NHP's core services include comprehensive homebuyer education classes designed to help prospective buyers navigate the home purchase process and avoid common pitfalls. They offer one-on-one financial coaching at no cost, including credit report review, error disputes, and credit corrections. The organization provides web-based homebuyer education through eHome America for 24/7 access, eliminating scheduling and transportation barriers. Additional services include financial literacy workshops, home repair grant and loan programs for income-eligible households, USDA loan packaging, foreclosure intervention with self-directed packets, and affordable housing development including senior rental properties.

NHP distinguishes itself through its NeighborWorks America affiliation, demonstrated by maintaining an exemplary rating within the national network. All counselors hold national HUD certification, ensuring compliance with federal housing counseling standards. The organization addresses accessibility by offering both in-person and online education options, and provides community education on predatory lending and foreclosure scams. Their mission explicitly focuses on partnership-based solutions combining residents, business, and government to build housing stability.

As a free non-profit service, NHP is most valuable for individuals seeking genuine housing education and financial counseling rather than loan products. The organization's strength lies in education and counseling rather than financial products; consumers should note that while home repair programs exist, the website content is incomplete regarding specific loan terms, income limits, or application processes for these programs.
pros: All three counselors hold national HUD certification ensuring federal compliance and expertise
Completely free one-on-one financial coaching including credit report review and dispute assistance
HUD-approved housing counseling agency with exemplary rating in NeighborWorks America network
24/7 web-based homebuyer education through eHome America eliminates scheduling and transportation barriers
Comprehensive financial literacy workshops covering budgeting, credit, homeownership readiness, and aging in place
Serves three-county Ohio region (Clark, Champaign, and contiguous counties) as NeighborWorks-chartered member
Offers both emergency home repair assistance and affordable housing development programs
cons: Website lacks specific details on income eligibility limits, loan amounts, or interest rates for home repair programs
Limited geographic service area restricted to Ohio (Clark, Champaign, and contiguous counties only)
No online application process or fee schedule publicly available for any programs
Website does not specify typical timelines for foreclosure intervention or home repair assistance approval
No information about wait times, capacity limitations, or whether programs are first-come-first-served
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Ohio seeking free education and guidance before purchasing
Homeowners facing foreclosure who need intervention counseling and documentation support
Individuals with credit challenges seeking free, certified counseling to dispute errors and rebuild credit
Low-to-moderate income households needing home repair grants or affordable housing options
diagnosis: NHP is best for Ohio residents seeking legitimate, free housing education and financial counseling from a certified, HUD-approved non-profit. The main caveat is that this is a counseling and education organization, not a lender—those needing actual loans or immediate cash should look elsewhere, and the website lacks detailed program specifications like income limits or application procedures.
services: HUD-approved housing counseling with certified counselors
One-on-one financial coaching at no cost
Credit report review and error dispute assistance
Financial literacy group workshops (budgeting, credit, homeownership readiness, aging in place)
Emergency home repair grant and loan programs
Predatory lending education and scam prevention
Community education on foreclosure scams and fraud protection
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
